quick_facts.label: Award and focus
quick_facts.value: A Ph.D. in Nursing is a doctoral research degree. Indian Nursing Council (INC) guidelines describe its purpose as preparing nurse scholars to develop and apply nursing knowledge, improve nursing education, research and practice, and disseminate nursing knowledge.

quick_facts.label: Typical entry qualification
quick_facts.value: Under INC’s revised guidelines for part-time Ph.D. Nursing enrolment, applicants need an INC-recognised M.Phil. Nursing, M.Sc. Nursing, or postgraduate nursing degree; an M.Sc. Nursing score of at least 60%; and three years of teaching or clinical experience after M.Sc. Nursing, while working within the university’s jurisdiction. Institution-specific rules can differ.

quick_facts.label: Duration
quick_facts.value: INC lists Ph.D. Nursing as 3–5 years. Its 2025 revised guidelines specify three years with a five-year maximum for full-time scholars, and five years with a seven-year maximum for part-time scholars; the 2026–27 INC–RGUHS National Consortium prospectus is part-time and requires thesis submission within five years, with a possible maximum of seven years subject to approval.

quick_facts.label: Assessment and completion
quick_facts.value: The INC framework includes a Pre-Ph.D. examination after one year, covering Research Methodology & Applied Statistics and Nursing Science & Theory Development, plus a seminar/term paper and proposal defence. Award follows thesis evaluation and successful viva voce.

quick_facts.label: 2026–27 National Consortium fee context
quick_facts.value: For the INC–RGUHS National Consortium’s 2026–27 prospectus, listed charges include ₹3,000 application fee, ₹4,000 registration fee, ₹15,000 Pre-Ph.D. examination fee, and ₹25,000 thesis examination fee. The annexure also lists a ₹75,000 non-refundable nodal-officer fee and ₹25,000 caution fee; fees are explicitly subject to change, so applicants should confirm the current notice before paying.

sections.heading: Eligibility after M.Sc. Nursing
paragraphs: For the INC’s 2025 Ph.D. Nursing framework, the relevant pathway for a working, part-time scholar is an INC-recognised M.Phil. Nursing, M.Sc. Nursing, or other postgraduate nursing degree, with at least 60% in M.Sc. Nursing. The framework also specifies three years of teaching or clinical experience after M.Sc. Nursing and employment in a teaching institution, hospital, community centre, or nursing department within the university’s jurisdiction.

The entry standard is not identical across every university. For example, the 2026–27 INC–RGUHS National Consortium documentation requires successful entrance-exam selection before registration and asks selected candidates for B.Sc. and M.Sc. Nursing credentials, a guide’s consent, a research proposal, an employer NOC where applicable, and State Nursing Registration Council authentication of RN&RM registration.
bullets: Confirm that the university and nursing programme are recognised/approved for Ph.D. Nursing before applying.
For a part-time route, confirm both experience and employer/NOC requirements early.
Check the current university or consortium prospectus, because its operational admission rules may be more specific than the INC baseline.

sections.heading: Duration and research timeline
paragraphs: The national regulator’s nursing-programme overview gives a broad Ph.D. duration of 3–5 years. INC’s 2025 revised guidelines distinguish full-time study (three years, maximum five) from part-time study (five years, maximum seven).

The INC–RGUHS National Consortium’s 2026–27 route is expressly part-time: it expects completion and thesis submission within five years of provisional registration, with a possible maximum of seven years subject to the required approvals. Scholars submit half-yearly progress reports; two consecutive late or unsatisfactory reports can lead to cancellation of provisional registration.
bullets: Full-time: 3 years; maximum 5 years under the INC 2025 guideline.
Part-time: 5 years; maximum 7 years under the INC 2025 guideline.
National Consortium route: part-time, ordinarily submit within 5 years; extension to a 7-year maximum is conditional.

sections.heading: Research-based curriculum and milestones
paragraphs: This is principally a supervised research degree rather than a taught clinical-specialisation programme. The required academic work combines research design, applied statistics, nursing science, theory development, literature review, ethics/permissions, a research proposal, data collection and analysis, thesis writing, external evaluation, and viva voce.

In the INC framework, confirmation of registration follows successful completion of the Pre-Ph.D. examination after one year. The specified components are two three-hour, 100-mark papers—Research Methodology & Applied Statistics and Nursing Science & Theory Development—plus a research-area seminar/term paper and proposal defence. The National Consortium prospectus requires at least 60% in each paper and 70% attendance for contact hours.
bullets: Pre-Ph.D. Paper I: Research Methodology & Applied Statistics.
Pre-Ph.D. Paper II: Nursing Science & Theory Development.
Internal assessment: seminar or term paper in the research area.
Proposal defence and final thesis viva voce.

sections.heading: Admission route
paragraphs: Admission is university- or consortium-administered. INC’s revised guidelines allow selection either on entrance-test merit or M.Sc. Nursing merit (with a minimum 60% in M.Sc. Nursing). Where a university uses an entrance test, the guideline specifies 90 entrance-test marks, with 54/90 as the minimum qualifying weightage, plus possible weightage for M.Phil. Nursing and publications.

The INC National Consortium page currently publishes a 2026–27 part-time Ph.D. Nursing application and prospectus. Its selected-candidate registration process requires a recognised guide, a proposed research title and synopsis, and—in-service candidates—guide consent and an NOC from the employing department/institution. Applicants should use the live notice rather than relying on a previous intake’s dates or seat availability.
bullets: Identify a recognised guide and an INC/consortium-approved study centre or a university-approved Ph.D. Nursing setting.
Prepare qualification records, registration authentication, proposal/synopsis, guide consent, and employer NOC if employed.
Follow the current entrance, merit-list, document-verification, and fee-payment notice for the intended university or consortium.

sections.heading: Fees: dated and provider-specific
paragraphs: There is no single India-wide Ph.D. Nursing tuition price. Charges depend on the university, study mode, location, hostel arrangements, research expenses, and whether the scholar is funded or employed. The 2026–27 INC–RGUHS National Consortium prospectus is useful as dated context rather than a universal price list.

That prospectus lists ₹3,000 for application, ₹4,000 for registration, ₹15,000 for the Pre-Ph.D. examination, and ₹25,000 for the Ph.D. examination submitted with the thesis. It additionally lists a ₹75,000 non-refundable nodal-officer fee, a ₹25,000 caution fee, and ₹30,000 per year for the sixth and seventh years. The prospectus states that RGUHS fees are subject to change.
bullets: Treat published fees as intake-specific, not as a nationwide benchmark.
Ask whether the quoted amount includes guide/study-centre, institutional, ethics-review, travel, statistical-software, fieldwork, thesis-printing, hostel, and extension costs.
Confirm payment method and refundability before submitting fees.

sections.heading: What the degree prepares graduates to do
paragraphs: INC describes doctoral nursing education as preparation for nurse-scholar roles that conduct relevant research, develop nursing theories and nursing science, synthesize nursing and allied-science knowledge, lead in nursing practice, education and research, and disseminate research findings. These outcomes align the degree with research-intensive academic, nursing-education, clinical-research, evidence-based-practice, and health-system leadership pathways.

The immediate next step after successful thesis evaluation and viva voce is award of the degree by the university. Career outcomes and hiring requirements remain employer- and institution-specific; candidates should therefore compare doctoral research areas, supervisor expertise, publications, and approved study-centre status against their intended academic or research path.
bullets: Academic nursing and postgraduate teaching/research roles.
Nursing and health-services research, including evidence-generation and dissemination.
Leadership roles involving nursing education, research, practice improvement, and policy-relevant research.
